title: Watch is great!  App sucks.
text: The physical product seems to work very well, so far.  The app that drives it was poorly written in China.  The company has removed their web page.  There appears to be NO support for the app at all.  (try clicking on the app's website from Google Play Store, and you'll get the dreaded 404)  Worst of all, the docs were written in Chinese and only marginally ported over to English.  Besides being VERY difficult to understand, the app doesn't work as advertised.  If you're quite comfortable with your Android phone and its OS, you should be mostly okay.  I get the feeling it MIGHT work a bit better with Apple phones.  If you want an inexpensive watch that does an okay job with BP, O2 and the rest, this may be your product.  If you're looking for customizability and depth of use, spend more and look elsewhere.

title: UPDATE: Budget-friendly watch but...
text: Updating previous review: Initially, this seemed like a great budget-friendly watch. However, after about two weeks I started to notice issues when connected to the watch app. I eventually had to remove the app from my phone because not only did it drain my cell battery daily, but it was causing my phone to ring and vibrate when no calls or texts were incoming. This was super annoying and happened repeatedly each day. Only when I removed the app did my phone behave normally again. The watch works but doesn't update the time/day automatically when a time change occurs. Dreading having to download the watch app again to update the time and day.

title: Not useful for those wanting to know heart rate during exercise.
text:
[[VIDEOID:9fabf79f503d7db7dcb8e446ba10dda0]] Pros: Heart rate accurate, O2 sat accurate, sleep tracker good & useful for determining resting HR, comfortable band, easy to read main screen & has interchangeable screens to suit your preference, easy navigating between functions. The app is easy to use and setting up the watch is also easy.
Cons: Exercise/sport settings include badminton, cricket, (really), boating, hockey & rugby. Who in their right mind would wear this glass face watch doing rugby or hockey? But there is no setting for weight training or elliptical, two common types of exercise & training. How many people playing badminton are really concerned about their testing heart rate ??
The blood pressure monitoring is way off as you can see from the photos, 125/64 with a calibrated sphygmomanometer & 142/84 with the watch, but I didn’t really expect an accurate blood pressure from a watch anyway.
Update: This fitness watch does not accurately measure heart rate du

title: Not impressive!
text:
I just got this over the past weekend I am really amazed how it's been working so far. I had those other types from other companies they cost so much yet I can't get SMS or SM for notifications on it anymore. Mine had Alexa built-in and GPS, being able to use the smart watch as "DICK TRACY" cartoon series was. But it got to a point that that feature causes a lot of problems. So let's keep it simple now.

Heart taker
High Blood Pressure Taker
Oxygen Taker
No Stress Taker (feature)
No Breathing (feature)
Sleep Taker (tracks all your sleeping no matter what time it is if your sleeping it will track it)

First picture you see is optional download through the app you install there are so many watch faces wow.
Second and Third is the default face.

Battery
So far so good

Vibrates no real sound in this.

5 Sensors on this one where the other brands just have 2, 3

I didn't give this one 5 stars sin

title: Nice and light
text: Beautiful. It is very hight. The quality is good. The color is beautiful. Also, it has many options.

title: Nicely designed display
text: I like the options for the display, lots of custom choices. The only negative I find with this watch is the alarm. There are only three times available and the vibration is weak. Sometimes it resets itself after 2 or 3 buzzes. I need 5 alarms and one that is stronger.

title: Could not get the watch to sync with Android App
text:
I cannot speak for everyone, but my experience was the following as an android user:
Pro: Watch arrived on time and in good condition. It was also very comfortable on the wrist--the strap is nice and soft. Hence the 2 star rating
Con: I could not get the watch to sync with the app. I loaded and unloaded several versions. None worked with my Samsung Galaxy. I was willing to forgo setting the time and syncing my contacts, email, text, etc as I really only wanted the fitness features. However, those features did not retain the settings either. Also the enclosed printed instructions were printed in a font size so small as to be unreadable and useless. All in all very disappointing. Returned.

title: Caution: major safety hazard!!!
text: [[VIDEOID:ccdf365dca066c7ae2057f91f94b6b7d]] Was wearing this watch when my skin started to burn. I took off the watch as quickly as possible. Took a regular thermometer to it and after climbing above 108 degrees, the thermometer wouldn’t read any higher. Then I put a meat thermometer on it (see video) reached 216 degrees! I’m so thankful I was awake when this happened.

title: Better than more expensive trackers...
text: It is dumb that a more comprehensive manual is not available if only as a PDF online.  A great product for the price, better than more expensive models I have used.

title: This watch is comically bad
text: I’m not mad I bought it, it’s cheap and I should have know it would be crap. It never tracked my sleep once in the 3 weeks I used it. Steps seem are low compared to Fitbit. It checks your blood pressure; awesome! Something my Fitbit doesn’t do. WHOOPS! Also not accurate. I tested my blood pressure with an arm cuff also and it was totally different. Bluetooth only reaches about 20 feet. Can’t even change settings in app if the phone is too far away from the device. It controls music, but doesn’t display the track or artist (not a big deal to me). It does not automatically track your workouts. It had weather on it, but it was for a different city and did not let me change it. It checks heart rate, but it’s spotty, and again, I don’t think it’s accurate. I gave it to my 8 year old daughter and bought myself a hundred dollar Fitbit. Worth it.

title: The app was loaded with ads.
text: I sent the watch back. I didn't even take it out of the box. I downloaded the app and it was loaded with ads. If you wanted it without ads you had to pay another $4.99 for it. I didn't want it with all the ads and I wasn't going to pay extra for another app.  I sent it back.

title: Nice looking "Watch"
text: It is a nice looking watch. However it only works as a watch sometimes. The remainder of the functions do not work. I walked to my kitchen and back to my bedroom in a typical sized home and it say i walked 2104 steps. Not. It says i have high blood pressure, however I don't, I checked with a real blood pressure machine. It says I have 99% oxygen, I don't. I have COPD w/emphysema, my oximeter says 91. The weather is extremely delayed. During the day it was 68 at night it still says 68, I had to use the phone app to get it to update the watch. It was 51. So then i went to sleep. Slept well, but when I woke up I didn't even have a screen to read. I had to hold the button down, turn it off and restart. Nope. I had to reset it.  This is not worth your money except for a watch, when the screen doesn't go blank. No not worth it. I would not recommend it. Or buy it. It will be a hassle to replace. So I lose.

title: Not meant for Working Men.
text:
The watch band broke 2 times. The first time my husband caught the metal piece that broke and I was able to put it back together. He wasn’t so lucky the 2nd time.
The app setup process was NOT easy. I’m a tech Savvy person and it was making ME mad…the return process was easy and we did get a refund.

title: Smart watch
text: This is a very good watch and the price was great. It does a great job of tracking sleep, steps, blood pressure, heart rate, and there is an app it can connect to on your phone. There are also other activities you can do with this watch that it has listed and it can connect to your music too. You can also receive text messages . Battery life is good.

title: Smart watch
text: This is a very good watch and the price was great. It does a great job of tracking sleep, steps, blood pressure, heart rate, and there is an app it can connect to on your phone. There are also other activities you can do with this watch that it has listed and it can connect to your music too. You can also receive text messages . Battery life is good.

title: Smart Watch
text: This watch I got for my dad for Christmas this year. Because the watch he had was at least 8 years old. I wanted him to have one like mine. He really likes his new watch. The way it tracks his sleep and how long he slept and his rem sleep. I am happy and so is my dad. Oh yes I have told my friends about your watch. Thank you for selling your products on Amazon.

title: It’s OK
text: I like the design, pretty stylish on a budget. However, it doesn’t notify me of messages. And I think the pedometer overestimates, as I’ve used a fitness tracker before and that one actually used a calibrate when setting up, this one does not. Ultimately returning, but if it worked properly, it’s an OK watch for the price.

title: It worked (kind of)... for about a month or two...
text: Say hello to your new paperweight. They put the charging port contacts on the back that go against your wrist. After about a month they start oxidizing. Now the device will not charge. USELESS! While it was working: the android app was quite wonky and was hit-or-miss on notifications. Truly you get what you pay for - also it is quite small to be considered unisex, it's size is more akin to a woman's wrist

title: Easy setup and works flawlessly
text: The app is user-friendly, step tracking, calorie count, heart rate, sleep monitoring; all works perfectly!

title: Battery life is great!
text: This works great for an inexpensive watch.  It is much easier to use than the last one I had.

title: Measurements are way off
text: The SPOX, BP and HR are all way off. other than that it is great.

title: Quit taking BP and blood pressure readings after a few months.
text: Cheap little smart watch, data measurein stopped working after a few months.

title: Phone and Text notifications don't work. Sleep tracking is worthless.
text:
Pros: A lot of the functionality appears to work, although I've not tried all the exercise options. Battery life is excellent -- after 8 days I still have 56%

Cons:

1 Phone and Text notifications do not work. I have Phone and Messages turned on in app. If I also turn on Other, I get worthless "Widget updating" notifications every 30 minutes, so SOME notifications work.

2 Sleep tracking is worthless. You can't specify when to start and stop; IT does, and does very badly. And the claimed amount of deep sleep is absurdly low. (This is not my first smartwatch, and the previous gave me numbers which seem far more accurate.)

3 Weather is broken. It worked initially but no more. Turning it off and on in the app did not help.

title: don't waste your money
text: very limited watch, difficult to setup no product support

title: It can only be used with a phone 9.5 or higher.
text: I don’t know if I like it or not because I have an 8.5 phone. So I cannot use it. It would be nice for them to notify people about that either before you buy it or in the advertisement. So I cannot tell if it’s any good

title: Great watch
text: This watch exceeds all of my expectations of a watch which I haven't had one on my are in decades

title: ordered second one for sister
text: This is a very nice watch with all the qualities of a expensive fitness monitor. colorful and screen size is nice. monitoreds o2 sats and b/p. Nice when your older and need to monitor..

title: Provides Basic Features
text:
This is my first smart watch. I purchased it to track basic functions, such as heart rate, steps taken, sleep, and such. Overall it is easy to set up, and it does the job reasonably well.

Blood pressure monitoring, however, is useless. The readings are consistently high and bear no resemblance to readings obtained with a regular blood pressure monitor. I've tried repositioning the watch and tightening and loosening the band, but those things have no effect on the erroneous readings. If this function would have worked, I would have rated the watch a '5', because BP monitoring was a function that is important to me. With this feature not working properly, the highest I can rate the watch is a '3'.

title: Son loves it
text: 17yo was very impressed with the features. Much cheaper than a Samsung watch. He is very happy with it and loves all the things it can do.

title: Not a good watch
text:
I've had the watch for 3 months! It never really tracked my steps well. My steps recorded on my samsung phone and the watch are always really far off, i.e. my watch would say I walked a total of 50 steps all day and my phone would say 1500 or whatever the case may be. The tracking for the heart rate, calories and so forth never really worked either! The app. never worked and it's hard to understand.
Just upgraded my phone and unable to link the phone because the find button on the watch is not working for some reason. I would choose another brand if I were you.

title: Good quality tracker
text: Love this tracker and seems pretty accurate. Disappointed that the oxometer does not seem to be on it…overall I’m pleased.. battery is good for 4 days without recharging.

title: Good quality tracker
text: Love this tracker and seems pretty accurate. Disappointed that the oxometer does not seem to be on it…overall I’m pleased.. battery is good for 4 days without recharging.

title: It stopped working after 2 weeks
text: I really loved it at first… and now I am so disappointed… it just stopped working after 2 weeks… the battery held great… and I love the looks of it and the things it could do… but I am returning it now…

title: Nice rest time peace but cheap
text: Nice time peace but cheap This piece kept very good time and was easy to read, all the other applications weren’t worth a hill of beans.  Pulse ox was off, heart rate was off, blood pressure was off. Everything you like this watch for, is off. Including the step counter.

title: Great looking watch. Just not compatible enough.
text: The watch itself is great. No, it doesn’t duplicate solid medical advice, but damn good.I just wish that it was compatible with anything other than Gloryfit (yuck!) If Gloryfit is good enough for you, then have at it.

title: Gift is pending
text: This is for a birthday present in June.....stay tuned!

title: Total crap
text:
Crappy watch, even for the price. Would NOT connect to the app they recommended, and there is no option to even use another, nor will the watch itself let you make any adjustments, not even the date or time, without the app. I sent it back for a refund, and that was the only good thing about it. I got my money back and if I had had a printer that worked, I wouldn't have had to leave the house.

A+ for Amazon, F- for the company that sells this garbage.

title: difficult to set all the functions.
text: This did not come with "easy to follow" instructions.  I went online to find a YouTube video but could not find the correct watch because I didn't have a "good" model number.  The ones I located on YouTube did not match this watch.  Frustrated, I sent it back.

title: Great for preteens!
text: Bought for active 12 year old teens! They are just enough for them! Iwatch who? Lol

title: Good product
text: Sleep monitoring and daytime fitness tracking

title: Good watch, a few problems
text: It was a dream to set up, no problems. One thing tho. Step counting is done by arm movement and not gps. I found when I was working and using my arms a lot it counted as steps. Like I was sanding some furniture and it counted as steps. I couldn't figure out why I had so many steps logged...lol. Then I did some tests with moving my arms only and got my step count to move in sinc with my arms. Also when hiking and using treking poles, I move them every other step and my step count reflected that. The sleep  thing is a bit off to. It would count just relaxing in a chair as light sleep, so I might show 10 or 11 hours of sleep.

title: Really good quality for price.
text: i love this watch it has every you need for a smart watch.
